item.title: FTA calls for pay rise for teachers and higher rural allowances
Police confirm to family that Kinoya man was under the influence of a substance during his arrest
item.description: Police have confirmed to the family of Sakiasi Ose Radravu, who died days after being arrested, that he was under the influence of a substance when he was arrested in Kinoya, claiming his behaviour at the time of the arrest was marked by distress, including screaming as officers took him into custody. The family confirmed this to fijivillage News, but for Radravu's family, those words have done li
item.content:encoded:
Police have confirmed to the family of Sakiasi Ose Radravu, who died days after being arrested, that he was under the influence of a substance when he was arrested in Kinoya, claiming his behaviour at the time of the arrest was marked by distress, including screaming as officers took him into custody.

The family confirmed this to fijivillage News, but for Radravu's family, those words have done little to ease the shock of what came next.

When fijivillage News enquired about the results of the post-mortem, they said they did not want to disclose the information.

The family member says the transformation from arrest to death was too sudden, too unexplained, and too difficult to accept without a full account of the events during that period.

Police have yet to publicly release detailed findings on the cause of death, and the matter remains under investigation.

item.title: Judgement not ready for King Khan's illicit drug possession case
item.description: The judgement for Imran Khan also known as King Khan for unlawful possession of illicit drugs is not ready.Khan was found with 4.1

item.title: Fear of FICAC is paralysing decision-making in the public sector - Halabe
item.description: Mark 1 Apparel Managing Director, Mark Halabe says the fear of FICAC is destroying public sector initiative and the current enviro

item.title: Man who hit father with cane knife and threatened mother given suspended sentence
item.description: The man convicted of striking his father on the head with a cane knife as well as threatening to stab his mother has been given a suspended sentence. Chand has been convicted with a count each of assault and criminal intimidation.
item.content:encoded:
The man convicted of striking his father on the head with a cane knife as well as threatening to stab his mother has been given a suspended sentence.

Chand has been convicted with a count each of assault and criminal intimidation.

The 23 year old has been sentenced to 8 months imprisonment, suspended for 3 years.

This means if he commits a crime within this period, he will be imprisoned.

Chand appeared before High Court Judge Justice Usaia Ratuvili this morning.

He has also been given a permanent DVRO to not make contact with his parents.

item.title: Sisilia Lora ensures safe school drop-offs as Howell Road Taxis controller
item.description: As a taxi controller for many years, Sisilia Lora has become more than just the voice behind the phones at Howell Road Taxis, she is a trusted figure for parents who rely on her to help ensure their children get home safely every day. Speaking on fijivillage Her-Story with Temalesi Vono, Lora says she spends her days juggling six ringing phones, coordinating drivers and managing bookings.
item.content:encoded:
As a taxi controller for many years, Sisilia Lora has become more than just the voice behind the phones at Howell Road Taxis, she is a trusted figure for parents who rely on her to help ensure their children get home safely every day.

Speaking on fijivillage Her-Story with Temalesi Vono, Lora says she spends her days juggling six ringing phones, coordinating drivers and managing bookings.

Lora says she regularly reminds drivers to look after the children in their care and make sure they enter their homes safely before leaving.

She says it is a routine she follows every school day, ensuring children travelling with the taxi service are not just transported, but looked after.

In an industry often dominated by men, she has earned the respect of colleagues through hard work, patience and perseverance.

Whether she is calming an upset customer, organising dozens of bookings or making sure a child gets through the gate safely, Lora says she approaches every task with kindness and care.

item.title: Urgent appeal for A positive blood donors to help 3-year-old Asinate Coriakula
item.description: The family of three-year-old Asinate Waicalanavanua Coriakula is urgently appealing to people with A positive blood type to donate blood as she fights for her life in the Burns Unit at CWM Hospital in Suva. Her doctors and loved ones are calling on members of the public with the same blood type to come forward and help give her a chance of recovery.
item.content:encoded:
The family of three-year-old Asinate Waicalanavanua Coriakula is urgently appealing to people with A positive blood type to donate blood as she fights for her life in the Burns Unit at CWM Hospital in Suva.

Her doctors and loved ones are calling on members of the public with the same blood type to come forward and help give her a chance of recovery.

Anyone willing to donate can contact her mother on 2742942.

item.title: Pilot rescued after Wailoaloa Beach crash
item.description: The pilot of the Robinson R44 helicopter was involved in an incident at around 5.23pm yesterday in waters off Wailoaloa Beach, nea
